evals.name: uc03-dataclass-ordering
evals.prompt: Can you check every Python snippet in plugin/skills/python/usecases/UC03-exhaustiveness.md and tell me which ones are broken? I'm reviewing a PR and I think there's something wrong with the dataclass examples but I want to be sure before I comment.
evals.expected_output: Report identifying the dataclass default-ordering bugs (snippets ~6, 7, 8, 19 — 'Fields without default values cannot appear after fields with default values'), plus a summary table. Should surface the bug clearly enough that the user doesn't have to dig through raw pyright output.

assertions.text: identifies-dataclass-ordering-bug: Response mentions 'Fields without default values cannot appear after fields with default values' or equivalent.
names-affected-snippets: Identifies at least 3 of the 4 affected dataclass snippets.
provides-line-numbers: Includes line numbers so the user can locate issues.
actionable-summary: Includes a summary or recommendation usable in a PR comment.

evals.name: t02-literal-and-bracket-mismatch
evals.prompt: Verify the code examples in plugin/skills/python/catalog/T02-union-intersection.md. There's supposedly a Literal[{...}] antipattern shown as a fix that I'm suspicious of.
evals.expected_output: Report catching: (1) the bracket-mismatch literal, (2) the TypedDict functional-form-as-type-alias misuse, (3) the Literal dict-as-value fix that is itself broken.

assertions.text: identifies-bracket-mismatch: Mentions the bracket/parenthesis mismatch in the Literal snippet.
identifies-literal-dict-misuse: Flags that Literal does not accept dict values.
confirms-user-suspicion: Directly addresses the user's question by confirming the Literal[{...}] usage is wrong.

evals.name: t05-abstractmethod-oneliner
evals.prompt: Run the verifier over plugin/skills/python/catalog/T05-type-classes.md. I skimmed it and some of the @abstractmethod code looks suspicious but I'm not sure if it's my eyes or a real issue.
evals.expected_output: Report flagging the @abstractmethod def on one line as syntax errors.

assertions.text: identifies-syntax-error: Flags that @abstractmethod def on one line is a syntax error.
names-affected-snippets: Identifies at least 1 of the 2 affected snippets.
explains-fix: Mentions decorators must be on their own line or shows correct form.

evals.name: clean-file-from-main
evals.prompt: Check the code in plugin/skills/python/catalog/T01-algebraic-data-types.md — this is the version that already landed on main, I just want a sanity check before I rebase.
evals.expected_output: Most snippets OK. Any failures should be noted as intentional antipattern demos.

assertions.text: mostly-clean: Communicates that most snippets pass or the file is generally in good shape.
contextualizes-failures: Notes that failures may be intentional antipattern demonstrations.
no-false-alarm: Does not alarm the user with urgent language about 'critical issues' for intentional demos.
